text: The surgeon begins a single-port ileocolic resection with mesenterectomy by creating a submesenteric tunnel, preparing the mesentery for resection.
Following the creation of the submesenteric tunnel, the surgeon takes the lateral peritoneal attachments to achieve lateral mobilization. The proximal transaction line is then marked on the bowel.
The surgeon identifies the ileocolic trunk, ensuring it is spared during the extended mesenterectomy. Once the position of the ileocolic trunk is confirmed, a mesenteric window is created.
The surgeon dissects the mesentery away from the ileocolic trunk, carefully continuing the dissection towards the marked proximal transection point along the trunk.
The surgeon continues the dissection alongside the ileocolic trunk towards the colonic transaction point, revealing the dissected triangular-shaped mesentery.
The surgeon closes the mesenteric defect using clips after completing the extracorporeal anastomosis, ensuring the mesentery is securely sealed.
